description: Trigger and behavior cases for security review of a real diff: boundary mapping, object-level/tenant-scope checks on SaaS paths, injection/secrets passes, exploit-path-gated severity, control-weakening detection.

cases.prompt: Security-review PR #212 before we merge — it adds a new endpoint that returns a customer's records.

assertions: Obtains the actual diff and records the command used, reviewing the change against the trust boundaries it touches.
Checks object-level authorization and server-derived tenant scope on the new data path even though the PR description may not mention them.
Runs injection, mass-assignment, and secrets passes and flags any weakening of an existing control.
Gives each high-severity finding an exploit path (persona → steps → payoff) and delivers an approve/request-changes verdict naming blockers.

cases.prompt: Review this small diff — it just broadens the CORS config and relaxes one auth check to fix a bug.

assertions: Flags the broadened CORS and relaxed auth check as security findings on their own, even though the feature works.
Assesses the exploit exposure the loosened control creates and requests changes or written risk acceptance rather than silently approving.

cases.prompt: Review this PR for correctness, readability, and test coverage.

assertions: General correctness/quality review is code-reviewer's job; security-pr-reviewer is the security-specialized pass, not the general one.

cases.prompt: I described the change I'm about to make in chat — just tell me if it's secure, no code yet.

assertions: Refuses to security-review from a description with no diff to obtain.
Suggests threat-modeler for design-time analysis and offers to review once an actual diff exists.
